clusters.title: Classical pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against MSO over Gaza comments – video
clusters.neutral_headline: Musician loses discrimination fight against orchestra over Gaza comments
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian Australia, ABC News Australia

items.title: Classical pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against MSO over Gaza comments – video
items.standfirst: A classical pianist who sued the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra has lost his case after he alleged he was unlawfully discriminated against because of his views on Israeli forces killing Palestinian journalists. Gillham had claimed the MSO’s cancellation of a Melbourne concert he was contracted to perform on 15 August 2024 was an attempt to silence him over his stance on Israel’s attacks in Gaza. Justice Graeme Hill handed judgment down on Friday, finding that the MSO would have taken the same actions had Gillham expressed a political belief in support of Israel. Pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against Melbourne symphony orchestra over Gaza comments Continue reading...
items.content: A classical pianist who sued the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra has lost his case after he alleged he was unlawfully discriminated against because of his views on Israeli forces killing Palestinian journalists. Gillham had claimed the MSO’s cancellation of a Melbourne concert he was contracted to perform on 15 August 2024 was an attempt to silence him over his stance on Israel’s attacks in Gaza. Justice Graeme Hill handed judgment down on Friday, finding that the MSO would have taken the same actions had Gillham expressed a political belief in support of Israel. Pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against Melbourne symphony orchestra over Gaza comments Continue reading...

items.title: Musician loses discrimination fight against orchestra over Gaza comments
items.standfirst: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.
items.content: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

items.title: This Place: Boonoo Moodla
items.standfirst: Boonoo Moodla, Billy Lights Point, has been a place of gathering and fishing for the Barngarla people for thousands of years. As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special. NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.
